items.title: Archived Maestro Estimate Docs & Introduced COGS Billing Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update includes the archiving of retired maestro cost-estimate design documents, which have been moved to a dedicated archive section. Additionally, a new billing design document has been introduced for maestro, transitioning from a duration-based formula to a model based on actual measured consumption (COGS), enhancing billing accuracy and transparency.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4.0 Message Handling and Fix Startup Issues in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two critical issues in WeChat 4.0: correcting message sender attribution and preventing startup hangs. The sender/direction attribution was fixed by aligning message database row IDs, ensuring accurate message display. Additionally, startup processes were optimized to avoid blocking calls, allowing the application to launch successfully even under slow conditions.

items.title: Enhance Auth Iframe Behavior in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update modifies the authentication iframe behavior by ensuring that the default providers remain visible on the main Platform host. Specifically, it prevents the passing of the `site` parameter when the iframe is opened on the official Platform, while still allowing white-label hosts to customize their branding. This change enhances user experience by keeping Google as a login option for the main Platform login test.

items.title: Enhance Web Login with Iframe Support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a feature flag for the web login process, allowing the reuse of the existing AuthPanel iframe when the `?features=auth-iframe` parameter is enabled. The changes ensure that the default login behavior remains unchanged while providing a seamless experience for users opting for the iframe method. Additionally, the protected-route guard has been updated to respect the new `auth-iframe` flag.

items.title: Enhance Login Experience with Iframe Feature Flag in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a feature-flagged iframe mode for the login process, allowing users to access the AuthFrontend directly within the PlatformFrontend. The legacy redirect flow remains the default, ensuring a smooth transition. Key changes include the addition of the AuthFrameDialog component and modifications to the routing logic to support the new iframe functionality.

items.title: Revamp Documentation for WeChat 4.0 Architecture in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update rewrites the Wisdom documentation to reflect the WeChat 4.0-only architecture, following the recent code de-versioning. Key changes include the removal of references to deprecated endpoints and modules, ensuring clarity for users. The README, SPEC, and SETUP documents have been thoroughly revised to align with the new architecture, enhancing usability and accuracy.

items.title: Fix revenue summary to retain normal orders in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue with the revenue summary API returning all zeros due to missing JSON keys in the metadata. The fix ensures that normal orders are retained when exemption keys are absent, only excluding those explicitly marked as true. This change restores a significant number of non-exempt orders, enhancing the accuracy of revenue reporting.

items.title: Streamline Login Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the dedicated `/auth/embed-login` route and allows the standard `/auth/login/` to display all configured login methods, whether framed or top-level. Additionally, it enhances security by implementing dynamic `frame-ancestors` in the Content Security Policy, preventing unauthorized iframes from receiving tokens. These changes improve both usability and security for users accessing the login page.

items.title: Retire Estimate/Start API Layer in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the cost-estimate and two-phase 'Start' API layer from Nexior as the feature is being retired. Key methods and interfaces related to estimates have been eliminated, streamlining the video creation process to directly post to '/maestro/videos'. All changes have been verified to ensure no UI call sites were affected, maintaining a clean type-check.

items.title: Retire Maestro Estimates from Auth Whitelist in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `/maestro/estimates` endpoint from the `SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS` in the settings file, reflecting the retirement of the maestro cost-estimate feature. As a result, both skip-auth and skip-record whitelisting for this endpoint are eliminated, ensuring no billing risks remain. The `/maestro/tasks` endpoint remains unaffected.

items.title: Revert startupProbe window to 2h for smoother installs in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the startupProbe window to a 2-hour threshold, ensuring that the appliance first-boot install can accommodate typical cold installs without interruption. The change restores the previous configuration, allowing for better handling of slow nodes and ISO tails during installation. Overall, this adjustment enhances the reliability of the deployment process.

items.title: Retire Cost-Estimate Feature from Maestro in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `/maestro/estimates` cost-estimate feature from the Maestro video service as part of a billing redesign. Key files such as `estimator.py` and `pricing.py` have been deleted, along with associated tests. The change streamlines the service by transitioning to actual-consumption metering, ensuring that legacy estimate tasks cannot be billed or re-run.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Support by Removing Legacy Code in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update eliminates all support for WeChat 3.x, consolidating the codebase to focus solely on WeChat 4.0. Key changes include the removal of deprecated files, endpoints, and dependencies, as well as renaming components to reflect the new standard. The impact is a cleaner, more efficient codebase that enhances maintainability and performance for users of WeChat 4.0.

items.title: Revamp Admin Attribution and Analytics in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the information architecture by separating admin attribution from distribution analytics. The revenue dashboard and distribution analytics have been restored to their original functionality, while a new dedicated route for marketing attribution has been introduced. This change enhances the clarity and usability of the admin console, ensuring historical data remains visible.

items.title: Revert web login iframe changes in Nexior 🔄
items.summary: This update reverts the recent changes made to the web login iframe functionality. Modifications were made to the AuthPanel and actions files, removing embedded iframe references and restoring previous code. The impact of this change aims to stabilize the authentication process for users.

items.title: Reverted login iframe integration for a cleaner auth experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the previous implementation of embedding a login iframe in the portal. The changes include significant reductions in the Login.vue file, removing unnecessary iframe-related code. This aims to streamline the authentication process and improve overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance CSP Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Content Security Policy for the embed login feature by using a backend-normalized `X-Frame-Ancestor-Origin` value. It retains the referer validation from previous changes while ensuring that the URL-encoded `embed_origin` does not appear in the CSP. This improves security and maintains proper functionality.

items.title: Enhance User Verification Display in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the user verification interface by hiding empty personal and enterprise verification fields, replacing the previous '未设置' fallback. The masking for ID card and phone fields remains intact when values are present, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Strengthen Embed Login Security in Frontend Web 🔒
items.summary: This update enforces the Content Security Policy for the embed login feature in production nginx, ensuring that only allowed origins can frame the content. It utilizes `auth_request` to validate the browser's `Referer` and responds with a 403 status when the parent origin is missing or unauthorized. These changes enhance security and protect against clickjacking attacks.

items.title: Enhance Site Domain Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `referer` as the preferred input for the frame ancestor API, ensuring that duplicate values are rejected. Additionally, it maintains origin normalization for full browser Referer URLs. These changes enhance security and improve the handling of frame ancestor requests.

items.title: Enhance frame ancestor header exposure in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `X-Frame-Ancestor-Origin` header to successful responses from the `/api/v1/site-domains/frame-ancestors` endpoint. This change allows the production nginx to utilize the backend-normalized origin for dynamic `frame-ancestors` Content Security Policy (CSP) configurations. The implementation has been validated with tests and is crucial for the upcoming AuthFrontend nginx CSP adjustments.

items.title: Refactor WeChat Read Adapter and Enhance Target Resolution in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update includes a rename of the WeChat read adapter for clarity and improved functionality. The new `resolve_target` method enhances the ability to map various identifiers to the canonical contact display name, ensuring accurate message sending. Additionally, the update drops unnecessary app-DB persistence for WeChat 4.0, streamlining the message handling process.

items.title: Enhance iframe policy endpoint for AuthFrontend in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new public endpoint at `/api/v1/site-domains/frame-ancestors` to manage iframe embedding policies for AuthFrontend. It allows first-party origins and tenant custom domains only when marked as active, while maintaining a separate Caddy TLS policy to prevent deadlocks. The changes enhance security and ensure a smoother user experience for embedded logins.

items.title: Enhance Web Login with Embedded Auth Iframe in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an embedded AuthFrontend iframe for web login, improving the user experience by allowing seamless authentication without leaving the white-label host. The existing native login flow is preserved, and strict validation measures are implemented to ensure security. Additionally, a Beachball patch change file has been added to support this feature.

items.title: Enhance Portal Login with Embedded Iframe in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the portal authentication redirect page with an embedded login iframe sourced from AuthFrontend. It ensures that logins are only accepted from the specified origin and iframe, while implementing a fallback to the legacy redirect if necessary. Key improvements include enhanced security measures and a smoother user experience for authentication.

items.title: Secure Embedded Login Route Added in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new `/auth/embed-login` route for email and phone-only embedded logins, enhancing support for white-label hosts. The Cloudflare Worker now injects a per-request `Content-Security-Policy` for this route, ensuring secure access. Existing login behaviors remain intact for full-page and OAuth flows, with fallback mechanisms in place for app-side consumers.

items.title: Boost UTM Attribution Dashboard for Marketing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the `/console/distribution-analytics` section by adding a new Marketing Attribution dashboard for admin users. It now displays detailed UTM source, medium, and campaign data, along with key metrics like signups and revenue. Additionally, UTM parameters are persisted on landing pages, improving tracking accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Marketing Attribution with New API in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new admin-only API endpoint at `/marketing-attribution/summary/` that groups signup cohorts by UTM parameters. It tracks signups to their corresponding usage and revenue, while also categorizing incomplete UTM data as 'unknown'. The changes improve data accuracy and provide clearer insights for marketing efforts.

items.title: Enhance User Signup with UTM Metadata in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to accept and store UTM metadata, including `utm_medium`, for new users during signup. It ensures that blank UTM fields are allowed to prevent malformed URLs from hindering the signup process. Additionally, the serializer has been updated to handle these changes defensively.

items.title: Enhance UTM Tracking for Signups in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update captures full UTM attribution parameters (`utm_source`, `utm_medium`, and `utm_campaign`) during the signup process in AuthFrontend. The changes ensure that UTM cookies are set atomically, preventing stale data from affecting new campaigns. This enhancement applies across various registration methods, including email, phone, and social logins.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Page Display in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the pricing page by addressing two main issues: it now properly formats the JsonLogic rules for better readability and removes duplicate entries for internal aliases. Key changes include the addition of a utility function to filter out synthetic alias cost rules and enhancements to the rendering of pricing formulas, making them clearer for users. These adjustments ensure a more user-friendly experience on the pricing page.

items.title: Enhance Revenue Exclusion Logic in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update excludes settlement-proof orders from the revenue summary when they are marked as revenue-exempt through metadata or tags. A new function has been added to streamline this exclusion process, and a regression test has been implemented to ensure the integrity of the revenue queries. These changes aim to improve the accuracy of revenue reporting.

items.title: Enhanced Verification Details Display in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a professional verification details card that appears after successful personal and enterprise verifications. Key fields such as ID card and phone numbers are masked to maintain privacy while still providing essential proof context. The changes enhance user experience by clearly presenting verification information.

items.title: Refine Admin Platform Rank Card Spacing in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update tightens the vertical spacing on the `/console/admin/platform-rank` page by removing duplicated margins from summary and podium cards. It introduces a new `row-gap` for better mobile card alignment and slightly adjusts the filter row separation for consistency. These changes enhance the overall layout and visual rhythm of the admin interface.

items.title: Enhance API Documentation with Export Options in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an 'Export API' dropdown in the API documentation header, allowing users to easily copy the OpenAPI URL, download the OpenAPI spec as JSON, and import it directly into Apifox or Postman. The dropdown is context-sensitive, appearing only when applicable, and supports multiple languages with i18n updates. Security measures have been implemented to ensure safe URL handling and clipboard operations.

items.title: Optimize WeChat-4 Appliance Startup Window in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: The startupProbe window for the WeChat-4 appliance has been tightened from 2 hours to 1 hour, adjusting the failureThreshold from 240 to 120. This change ensures a faster response to stuck boots while still allowing adequate time for a cold Win10 installation, which typically takes around 40 minutes. The update enhances efficiency without compromising the installation process, as pods can resume from persistent storage if restarted.

items.title: Fix billing profiles display issue in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the billing profiles list remained empty despite the API returning data. The parsing logic has been improved to handle responses containing `items` or `results`, ensuring that profiles are correctly populated. The fix enhances the user experience by displaying the correct billing profiles.

items.title: Enhance user menu with invoice icon in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update registers the invoice icon in the user menu, which previously displayed without an icon due to a missing entry in the Font Awesome library. The change ensures a more complete and visually appealing user interface. The code has been verified and is clean.

items.title: Documentation Update: Custom Domain Coverage in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the white-label documentation, specifically enhancing the section on custom domains. The coverage for custom domains has been successfully implemented, with internal redirects now utilizing location.origin. A known issue remains with the order/download footer, which is noted for future attention.

items.title: Fix chat opening issue in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue in WeChat where sending messages to closed group chats failed due to incorrect search handling. The search function now correctly identifies the chat box and clicks the appropriate result instead of pressing ENTER, which previously triggered an unwanted web search. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended.

items.title: Fixed English WeChat QR Login Detection in Wisdom 🔐
items.summary: Updated QR page detection to recognize the English "Scan to log in" label used by WeChat 4.0, preventing login QR requests from being misclassified. The change resolves failures in the authentication QR endpoint on fresh English installations by prioritizing the correct QR anchors. A new test covers the reported OCR output to verify the fix and prevent regressions.

items.title: Made Billing Profiles Always Visible in Frontend Web 🧾
items.summary: The billing profiles navigation item is now always visible by removing the invoice feature flag check. This makes the existing billing page consistently accessible from the user navigation and also cleans up the unused feature flag import. The change was verified with linting and type checking.

items.title: White-Label Progress Moves Forward in Index 📈
items.summary: The white-label progress tracker was updated to reflect that Wave 1 has been fully merged. It also records Wave 2 review status, with pending reviews highlighted for the remaining work. The documentation now provides a clearer snapshot of completed milestones and the current review queue.

items.title: Enhance White-Label Footer Customization in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update allows the white-label developer portal to hide our legal identity in the footer, including company name and ICP registration, while maintaining default behavior when branding is not configured. Key modifications include updates to the `ISiteBranding` interface and the footer component to conditionally render branding information based on new flags. This ensures a cleaner presentation for white-label users without compromising existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance White-Label Sharing Links in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the distribution sharing links to prevent the exposure of the `share.acedata.cloud` domain for white-label sites. Instead, these sites will retain a neutral short URL, while official sites will continue to use the branded domain. The change involves a simple logic adjustment in the code, ensuring a seamless experience for both types of users.

items.title: Enhanced Invoice Dialog and Order List Features in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update refines the invoice application dialog by centering its layout and improving the eligibility message for clarity. Additionally, a new button has been added to the order list for eligible orders, allowing users to apply for invoices directly from the list. These changes enhance user experience and ensure accurate information is presented.

items.title: Enhance Order List with Active Invoice Details in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the order list API by integrating active invoice information directly into each order row. The `OrderIndexSerializer` now includes a method to fetch the active invoice's brief details, improving efficiency by eliminating unnecessary data fetching on the frontend. Additionally, the queryset is optimized to prefetch active invoices, ensuring no N+1 query issues arise.

items.title: Unify Stripe Webhook Paths for Consistency in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Stripe webhook paths for both orders and invoices to a consistent format of `/<resource>/stripe/webhook/`. The invoice path has been modified, and the order detail action has been refactored to eliminate magic strings. This change enhances clarity and maintainability while ensuring existing functionality remains intact.

items.title: Enhance Invoice Header Form Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the invoice header form based on user feedback, addressing issues such as empty states and field order. Key improvements include mandatory fields for special invoices, enhanced auto-completion for registered headers, and a more user-friendly layout for international addresses. Additionally, the update ensures compliance with internationalization standards across multiple languages.

items.title: Update on White-Label Progress in Index 🚀
items.summary: The white-label README has been updated with the latest progress on key items. Notably, the Nexior branding features are under review, including the ability to hide branding elements like 'About' and 'View Code'. This update ensures that all five items in the first wave have corresponding PRs, with one already merged and others pending review.

items.title: Enhance Login Branding for Resellers in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces per-site branding for the SSO login page, allowing resellers to display their own title, favicon, and theme color. Key changes include the addition of site-specific theme handling in the login interface, ensuring a seamless branding experience without altering the default settings for the bare auth domain. The implementation is designed to maintain compatibility with existing features and enhance user experience.

items.title: Empower Resellers to Hide Branding in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces functionality allowing resellers to hide AceDataCloud branding in Nexior. Key changes include the addition of new types for site branding, modifications to the About and View Code components, and the implementation of helper functions to manage branding visibility. The default behavior remains unchanged, ensuring a seamless experience for users who do not configure branding settings.

items.title: Pin WeChat Version to Prevent Automation Breakage in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new PowerShell script to disable the WeChat auto-update feature, ensuring that the application remains on a validated version. Key changes include modifications to the Dockerfiles and the addition of scripts that prevent the installation of new versions, thereby protecting the automation functionalities of Wisdom. The implementation has been validated live, confirming that the running client remains unaffected while blocking unwanted updates.

items.title: Fix transient header read issue in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a false-negative issue when sending messages in the WeChat integration, specifically when the chat header briefly returns `None` after a prior send. The fix enhances the `_open_and_verify_title()` function to differentiate between transient reads and genuine wrong chats, ensuring messages are sent correctly. Additionally, the retry mechanism for reading chat titles has been improved, increasing the number of attempts to enhance reliability.

items.title: Fix English Shortcuts Recognition for Group Creation in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the English version of WeChat 4.0 was unable to recognize the 'Shortcuts' label in the group creation menu. By adding 'Shortcuts' to the `_QUICK_ACTION_LABELS`, the functionality is restored, allowing users to successfully initiate group chats. Additionally, a new test has been implemented to ensure this path works correctly.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Moments with Full CRUD Functionality in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive CRUD interface for WeChat Moments, allowing users to publish, read, and delete their posts via the API. Key features include the ability to publish text and images, retrieve a list of Moments from the local database, and safely delete posts with content verification. The implementation ensures a seamless user experience while maintaining data integrity.

items.title: Progress Update: White-Label Items in Review and Merged in Index 🚀
items.summary: Wave 1 is making strides with item 00 now in review, focusing on the Site.branding column. Additionally, item 17, which addresses per-service pricing, has been successfully merged. These updates enhance our white-label capabilities and improve overall functionality.

items.title: Enhance Site Branding with New JSON Field in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new validated JSON field, `Site.branding`, to support per-site branding configurations. Key changes include the addition of a validator and a migration for the new field, ensuring that site owners can customize their branding without affecting existing data. Comprehensive testing has been conducted to ensure stability and functionality.

items.title: Enhance Task Failure Attribution in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces per-task failure attribution to the maestro worker, enhancing observational logging without altering rendering behavior. It allows for clearer identification of failure causes, such as container-limit OOM or time budget issues, aiding in data-driven decision-making for resource adjustments. The implementation is purely additive and has passed all tests successfully.

items.title: Enhance HappyHorse API Pricing Visibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical hotfix for the HappyHorse pricing page by ensuring that both 720P and 1080P API cost rows are visible. The changes include modifications to the API cost JSON file and the addition of tests to validate the correct display of pricing. The fix has already been applied to the production database, enhancing the user experience on the pricing tab.

items.title: Progress Update: Per-Service Pricing Bug Fix Submitted in Index 🚀
items.summary: The per-service pricing bug fix for item 17 has been submitted for review in the PlatformBackend repository. The README progress has been updated to reflect this change, moving from red to yellow. Future updates will continue to track the completion of wave tasks.

items.title: Fix per-service pricing bug for distributors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical bug where the per-service markup for distributors was not being applied during order billing. Key changes include the introduction of a new function to determine effective markup ratios and modifications to the order processing logic to ensure that per-service overrides are correctly utilized. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate these changes and ensure the integrity of the billing process.

items.title: Exciting New HappyHorse CLI Package Added in Clis 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces the HappyHorse CLI package, enabling users to generate AI videos from text, images, and reference images via the AceDataCloud platform. Key commands include 'generate', 'image-to-video', and 'video-edit', along with comprehensive task management features. All OpenAPI parameters are now accessible, enhancing the CLI's functionality and user experience.

items.title: Inject STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET for Invoice Automation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable into the main container of the platform-backend. This change ensures that the Stripe webhook for the invoice system can verify signatures and automate invoicing without disrupting existing functionalities. The deployment has been tested successfully, confirming that the webhook now returns the correct status code.

items.title: Launch credential-as-authorization feature in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The `credauth` feature flag has been removed, making the credential-as-authorization UI fully available. Key changes include the removal of conditional gates in various components, ensuring that the Authorize page and buttons are always visible. This update streamlines the user experience without altering backend authorization processes.

items.title: Enhance Service API Mapping Documentation in Index 📚
items.summary: This update significantly improves the documentation for the service API mapping process, particularly emphasizing the importance of the `service_api_mapping.json` file as the entry point for the public `/models` AI Model Directory. Key changes include a rewrite of Step 22 to clarify the consequences of missing submissions and additional notes on media services and their branding requirements. The documentation now also addresses previously overlooked pitfalls related to syncing and media service branding.

items.title: Revamp White-Label Strategy with 23 Detailed Docs in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive breakdown of the white-label project into 23 actionable documents, aimed at ensuring that end-users and developers feel no presence of AceDataCloud. Key changes include the addition of a structured README, detailed site configuration schema, and specific implementation plans for branding, custom domains, and API management. These enhancements are designed to improve user experience and streamline the deployment of white-label solutions.

items.title: Revamp Recharge Action Color in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The recharge action color for the service hero has been restored to a vibrant danger/red hue, enhancing visibility and user experience. Other actions maintain their compact sizing and primary/secondary styling. This change ensures consistency and clarity in the service interface.

items.title: Add HappyHorse Video Model to Models Directory in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of the HappyHorse video model not appearing in the /models directory. Key changes include adding HappyHorse to the service_api_mapping.json and updating catalog.py to ensure proper display. The catalog now correctly returns seven HappyHorse video cards, enhancing user access to this model.

items.title: New OpenAPI Export Endpoint for Easy API Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new public, read-only endpoint that provides a complete OpenAPI 3.x document for any public API documentation page. This allows users to easily import API specifications into tools like Postman and Apifox, enhancing accessibility and usability. Key changes include the addition of the `wrap_openapi()` function to ensure valid OpenAPI files and improved localization for international users.

items.title: Enhance Language Resolution for JSON Responses in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new language resolution mechanism in the JSONLocalizationRenderer, prioritizing the `?lang=` query parameter over the `Accept-Language` header. This allows users to specify a language directly in the URL, improving usability for scenarios like OpenAPI exports. Additionally, tests have been added to ensure the correct precedence and normalization of language selections.

items.title: Streamline Admin Navigation with Collapsible Group in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: The admin console's left navigation has been revamped to group superuser-only entries under a single collapsible 'Admin' header, enhancing usability. This change includes an auto-expand feature for active routes, a red badge for pending tasks, and improved keyboard accessibility. The update maintains existing functionality for non-admin users and ensures internationalization across all supported locales.

items.title: New Invoice System Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive invoice management system on the frontend, including a new invoice application dialog and an admin management page. Users can now apply for invoices directly from the order details page, while admins gain enhanced control over invoice statuses and management. The feature is controlled by a feature flag for gradual rollout.

items.title: Enhance Invoice Metadata Handling in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates order metadata into the auto-generated invoice objects within the Stripe payment process. By adding `invoice_data.metadata` to the `invoice_creation` parameters, the system ensures that the PlatformBackend can reliably link invoices back to their corresponding orders. This change specifically impacts the web PaymentLink functionality, while leaving intent and automatic recharge processes unaffected.

items.title: Exciting New Invoice System Features in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive backend for the invoice system, including user and admin APIs, Stripe webhook integration, and refund linkage. Key changes include the creation of new models for invoices and billing profiles, along with a robust service layer for handling invoice eligibility and status transitions. The implementation ensures a seamless experience for both users and administrators while maintaining data integrity and security.

items.title: New Independent Invoice Entry Page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated entry page for invoice titles at `/user/billing`, enhancing user experience by separating it from the profile section. It includes two tabs for China and overseas users, allowing for multiple invoice titles with options to add, edit, or delete. The implementation also supports internationalization across several languages and is controlled by a feature flag.

items.title: Enhance Billing Profiles for Invoice Management in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `BillingProfile` model to manage invoice titles and addresses, supporting multiple entries per user with region-specific validations. New API endpoints for creating, updating, and deleting billing profiles have been implemented, ensuring secure access for owners only. The changes include comprehensive unit tests and migration scripts, enhancing the overall functionality of the billing system.

items.title: Enhance Usage List with Credential ID Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Credential ID' column to the usage list, positioned just before the created time. It displays the credential name when available, or the full credential ID otherwise, improving clarity for users. Changes have been made to both the API and Proxy usage tables, ensuring consistent labeling across languages.

items.title: Enhanced Credential Visibility in Nexior 🌟
items.summary: This update introduces a new column for credential visibility in Nexior's usage list, allowing users to see the credential name alongside the usage records. The change applies to both API and Proxy tables, enhancing usability and clarity. Additionally, the new feature is localized across 18 languages, ensuring accessibility for a diverse user base.

items.title: Enhance Documentation Links for Services in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the documentation links on the introduction pages for the services Fish, Flux, Grok, and Maestro. The '查看文档' button now directs users to the respective service's primary API documentation in a new tab, improving user experience and accessibility. No frontend changes were made, ensuring a seamless integration.

items.title: Enhance Skills with API Validation and i18n Backfill in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two powerful skills: `validate-live-api` for end-to-end checks on the AceDataCloud API, and `i18n-backfill` to ensure all locales are updated with new keys from zh-CN. The `validate-live-api` skill includes features like response shape validation and JSON/Markdown reporting, while `i18n-backfill` automates the addition of missing keys without disrupting existing translations. Both skills have been thoroughly tested and are ready for deployment.

items.title: Streamline Service Hero Action Buttons in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the service hero action buttons by compacting them to fit 3-4 actions in a single row on desktop. The two-variant color system is retained, and titles are added to buttons for better accessibility. Additionally, the margin for adjacent buttons has been reset to improve spacing control.

items.title: Fix global search issue in Wisdom 🔍
items.summary: This update resolves a problem where sending messages to contacts not in the recent session list failed due to the incorrect search box being used. The `_find_search_edit` function now correctly identifies the top-left global search box, ensuring successful message delivery even when the group chat-info drawer is open. All relevant tests have passed, confirming the fix's effectiveness.

items.title: Fix WeChat search result selection issue in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where the WeChat search box sometimes opened the web search page instead of the intended chat. The fix ensures that the search function now correctly identifies and skips the web search result, preventing it from being selected. Additionally, new tests have been added to verify that the web search row is ignored during selection.

items.title: Enhance Seedream Service Intro with 5.0 Updates in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enriches the Seedream service introduction by adding the new 5.0 Pro and Lite models, along with updated pricing information. The prices have been adjusted to reflect current rates, ensuring accuracy and consistency with live billing. Additionally, the documentation now features enhanced model tags and a revised FAQ section to guide users effectively.

items.title: Complete Invoice System Design for Clients in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design for an invoice system aimed at clients wishing to issue invoices. Key features include a billing profile stored in the authentication database, a six-state status machine for invoice processing, and integration with Stripe for automated invoicing. The design addresses five user requirements and rectifies ten overlooked rules, ensuring a robust and compliant invoicing process.

items.title: Redirect Empty Container Docs to First Child in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of empty pages rendering for container documents by implementing a redirect to their first child. The changes in `Detail.vue` ensure that when a container document is accessed, it now fetches and displays the first child inline without additional requests. This enhancement improves user experience by preventing blank pages and streamlining document navigation.

items.title: Enhance Document Detail Response with Public Children in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `DocumentChildSerializer` that returns public, rank-ordered children inline for container documents. By optimizing the detail response, it eliminates the need for a second request, improving performance and user experience. The changes ensure that only lightweight fields are loaded, maintaining efficiency while preventing private child leakage.

items.title: Updated Seedream API Docs to Remove Invalid Model ID in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the invalid dot-form model ID `doubao-seedream-5.0-lite` from the Seedream API documentation. The change ensures that only the accepted canonical IDs are listed, aligning with the PlatformService `ALLOWED_MODELS` and improving clarity for users. This is a documentation-only update that enhances the accuracy of the API reference.

items.title: Streamline Seedream Model IDs for Compatibility in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update removes invalid dot-form model IDs from the SeedreamModel, ensuring compatibility with the upstream worker. The changes include retaining only the six canonical date-suffixed IDs that are accepted, thus preventing HTTP 400 errors. The modifications also clean up related documentation and tools, enhancing overall functionality without affecting valid inputs.

items.title: Fix Seedream 5.0 Pro Parameter Injection Issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical bug affecting the Seedream 5.0 Pro, which was failing due to unsupported parameters being injected into requests. The fix ensures that parameters like `sequential_image_generation` and `stream` are only forwarded for models that support them, allowing the Pro model to function correctly while preserving behavior for other models. A thorough verification process confirms that existing functionalities remain intact.

items.title: Streamline Action Button Styles in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update simplifies the action button styling in the service hero section to a two-variant visual system. The primary action now uses the site’s main color, while a translucent secondary style is applied to other buttons. Additionally, the pricing action has been removed for clarity, and button sizes have been standardized for better mobile responsiveness.

items.title: Exciting addition of Seedream 5.0 Pro model in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Seedream 5.0 Pro model to the Seedream CLI, enhancing the model options available for image generation and editing. Key changes include updates to the output format help notes, README documentation, and test cases to reflect the new model. The integration ensures a seamless experience for users looking to leverage the latest capabilities of the Seedream CLI.

items.title: Enhance Seedream API with Pro Model in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Seedream 5.0 Pro model to the Seedream API documentation. Key changes include updates to the README and the integration guide, ensuring users can access the new model in their requests. This is a documentation-only change aimed at improving user experience.

items.title: Introducing the Seedream 5.0 Pro Model in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: We've added the Seedream 5.0 Pro model to the Seedream MCP server, enhancing our offerings with a flagship single-image generation option. Key updates include modifications to the model types, updated documentation, and a revised feature-comparison matrix. This addition aims to provide users with the highest quality output for single-image tasks.

items.title: Introducing Seedream 5.0 Pro Model Option in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Seedream 5.0 Pro model option to the Studio Seedream configuration. Key changes include the addition of a new model constant, updates to the capabilities matrix, and localization support across 17 languages. This enhancement allows users to select the Pro model, which is optimized for single image output.

items.title: Exciting Seedream 5.0 Pro Pricing and API Updates in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Seedream 5.0 Pro pricing structure, including tiered costs based on output pixels and additional charges for extra input images. Key changes include modifications to the API cost rules, the addition of new test cases, and updated documentation for the Pro model. These enhancements aim to improve user experience and ensure accurate billing for the new features.

items.title: Introducing Seedream 5.0 Pro with Tiered Billing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Seedream 5.0 Pro model to the volcengine worker, enhancing the output capabilities with tiered billing metadata. Key changes include the addition of the Pro model to allowed models and output formats, along with new metadata emissions for billing purposes. The integration ensures compatibility with existing cost rules while maintaining a clean and efficient billing process.

items.title: Enhanced Service Documentation and Demos in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update backfills the `Service.doc_url` and `Service.demo_url` fields for public services, adding 29 documentation URLs and 23 demo URLs. The migration ensures that only empty fields are filled, preserving existing data. All URLs have been verified for accuracy, enhancing the accessibility of service information.

items.title: Enhance Service Actions with New Links in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the single-purpose CTA area in the service hero with a unified action group, showcasing explicit service actions for documentation, pricing, online experience, and balance top-up. Localized labels have been added across all supported languages, ensuring a consistent user experience. Key behaviors have been refined to display actions based on available service data, improving navigation and usability.

items.title: Enhance Usage Records with Credential Names in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Credential' column in the console usage list, allowing users to easily identify which API key generated each usage record. The display shows the credential name, with a fallback to a shortened ID for cases where the name is not available. Additionally, the change includes updates to internationalization files to support this new feature across multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance API Usage Records with Credential Info in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a read-only `credential` object to the API usage serializers, allowing users to see which API key generated each usage record. The change improves readability by displaying the credential name instead of the raw UUID. Additionally, a new helper function has been added to facilitate this serialization.

items.title: Update homepage uptime display to fixed value in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update hardcodes the homepage service availability stat to '99.9%', ensuring a consistent uptime display. It retains dynamic updates for calls, active users, active APIs, and the as-of date from the public usage snapshot, while removing the previous dependency on the API usage file. The changes enhance the reliability of the homepage metrics without affecting the underlying data fetching logic.

items.title: Smooth Scroll to Tab Content on Deep Links in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances user experience by automatically scrolling to the specified tab content when deep-linking to a service page with a query parameter. The `ServiceDetail` component now includes a smooth scroll feature that activates only once per session for deep links, ensuring users can easily access the relevant information without manual scrolling. Key modifications include the addition of a reference for the tab section and a new flag to manage the scroll behavior efficiently.

items.title: Revert payment button changes in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the previous changes made to disable the Stripe payment button and the associated outage alert. Key modifications include the removal of localized messages regarding Stripe's temporary unavailability across multiple languages. The changes also affect the payment selection logic in the console and order pages, restoring previous functionality.

items.title: Enhance Group Creation and Renaming in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an atomic method for creating groups with an optional name, eliminating the need for name-guessing during the renaming process. Additionally, it fixes a false-negative issue in the renaming verification, ensuring accurate title updates. Robustness improvements have also been made to handle intermittent title reads more effectively.
